name: devflow-constitution-quick-ref
description: Quick reference guide to CC-DevFlow Constitution v2.0.0 with links to full text. Covers all 10 Articles and Phase -1 Gates.
capabilities: Summarize all 10 Constitutional Articles with key rules and examples
List Phase -1 Gates enforced by the planner agent before EPIC generation
Provide quick lookup answers for common development scenarios
Link to full Constitution document for detailed reading
author: Dimon94
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown reference material for CC-DevFlow Constitution. Static scanner detected patterns in markdown code examples as security risks, but all findings are false positives. The skill has no executable code, network calls, or file system operations. All 58 static findings were evaluated and dismissed as documentation artifacts.
valueStatement: Teams struggle to remember and apply development workflow rules consistently. This skill provides quick access to all 10 Constitutional Articles, Phase -1 Gates, and common scenario answers from the CC-DevFlow Constitution v2.0.0.
limitations: Does not contain the complete Constitution text (see project-constitution.md for full details)
Does not provide implementation guidelines for specific agents
Does not execute any validation or enforcement actions
Does not modify or create any files

useCases.title: Review Project Requirements Against Constitution
useCases.description: Before generating EPICs, verify your project scope meets Phase -1 Gates (simplicity, anti-abstraction, integration-first testing requirements).

useCases.title: Check Code Quality Rules During Development
useCases.description: Quick lookup of Article I (Quality First) and Article II (Architectural Consistency) rules when writing or reviewing code.

useCases.title: Resolve Common Development Questions
useCases.description: Get instant answers to questions like 'Can I add TODO for later?' or 'Should I create BaseController?' based on constitutional rules.

promptTemplates.title: Quick Article Lookup
promptTemplates.prompt: What does Article [I-X] of the DevFlow Constitution say about [topic]?
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick lookup of specific constitutional rules

promptTemplates.title: Phase -1 Gate Checklist
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the Phase -1 Gates that the planner agent checks before generating an EPIC?

promptTemplates.title: Scenario-Based Guidance
promptTemplates.prompt: Can I [specific action]? Refer to the DevFlow Constitution for the answer.
promptTemplates.scenario: Checking if a specific development action is allowed

promptTemplates.title: Rule Summary by Article
promptTemplates.prompt: Summarize the key rules for Article [I-X] with examples of what violates the constitution.

bestPractices: Use this skill as a quick reference before starting new work to ensure compliance with constitutional rules
Cross-reference with the full Constitution document (.-constitution.md) for detailed explanations
Applyclaude/rules/project Phase -1 Gates checks early in project planning to avoid scope and architecture issues later
antiPatterns: Do not assume the full Constitution text is contained here; always check project-constitution.md for complete rules
Do not use this skill to bypass constitutional enforcement mechanisms like guardrails
Do not implement features without checking relevant Articles first

faq.question: Does this skill enforce the Constitution rules?
faq.answer: No. This skill only provides reference information. Enforcement happens through guardrails like constitution-guardian and devflow-tdd-enforcer, and Phase -1 Gates in the planner agent.

faq.question: Where is the full Constitution text?
faq.answer: The complete Constitution is in .claude/rules/project-constitution.md. This skill provides summaries and links only.

faq.question: How does this skill differ from the constitution-guardian?
faq.answer: This skill is a reference guide that provides information. The constitution-guardian is a guardrail that actively blocks code that violates constitutional rules.

faq.question: What are Phase -1 Gates?
faq.answer: Phase -1 Gates are checks performed by the planner agent BEFORE generating EPIC and TASKS. They enforce Articles VII (Simplicity), VIII (Anti-Abstraction), and IX (Integration-First Testing) early in the planning process.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to check if my code is compliant?
faq.answer: You can use this skill to understand what the rules are, but actual compliance checking is done by validation scripts like validate-constitution.sh and code review agents.

faq.question: What happens if I violate the Constitution?
faq.answer: Violations are blocked by real-time guardrails during development, caught by validation scripts during batch checks, or flagged during code review. Specific consequences depend on the article and enforcement mechanism.
